3. Click on orange at (14,27): white cursor moved to (14,28), orange moved to (14,28)
4. Click on orange at (14,28): no impact
So the orange object moved from (12,24) to (14,27) to (14,28), and now it's stuck. The white cursor is at (14,28) following the orange object.
Wait, actually looking more carefully:
- After click 1: orange at (14,27), white cursor at (12,24)
- After click 2: orange at (14,28), white cursor at (14,25) (based on adjacency)
Hmm, let me re-examine. The white cursor position is (14,25) W based on the adjacency list. But I was tracking it as (32,0) W which is the HUD band.
Let me check the current state more carefully.

    cc = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])

</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
